name: KAMA Vortex Alignment Entry
edge_hypothesis: KAMA Stoch is the #2 promoted family (PPR 4.268) and Vortex transition is the PF champion (2.034). The prior kama_vortex_divergence spec (0/9 WR) failed because it looked for DIVERGENCE between KAMA and Vortex — a rare and noisy signal. This spec tests the OPPOSITE thesis: ALIGNMENT. When Vortex detects a momentum transition (VTXP crosses above VTXM) AND KAMA confirms the direction (close > KAMA), both the adaptive-speed baseline and the directional momentum indicator agree. KAMA's key property is speed-adaptation — it smooths aggressively in noise (slow) and follows closely in trends (fast). This natural noise filter should eliminate choppy Vortex false crosses without requiring an explicit regime gate like CHOP or ADX. The combined signal fires only when two mathematically independent adaptive systems agree: Vortex sees directional momentum shifting, KAMA sees price committed to that direction. This is a fusion of the two strongest non-supertrend families in the promoted library.

variants.name: kama_vortex_align_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core variant. Vortex transition + KAMA trend confirmation. 8:1 R:R (12x ATR TP, 1.5 ATR stop). Tests whether KAMA's adaptive filtering eliminates the choppy false crosses that raw Vortex produces, while preserving the transition-detection edge that makes Vortex the champion family.

components.notes: Kaufman Adaptive MA — speed-adapts to market noise. Fast in trends, slow in ranges. Natural choppy-market filter without explicit regime gate.
Vortex Positive — crosses above VTXM to detect bullish directional momentum transition.
Vortex Negative — crosses above VTXP for bearish momentum transition.

variants.name: kama_vortex_align_v1_10to1
variants.description: Extended TP variant. 10:1 R:R (15x ATR TP). Vortex champion family historically benefits from wider TPs because transition moves tend to be large (trans PF=3.886 on v3a). Tests whether KAMA-filtered entries produce even cleaner transitions that ride further.

components.notes: KAMA adaptive baseline — filters noise, lets transitions ride.
Vortex transition signal for long entries.
Vortex transition signal for short entries.

variants.name: kama_vortex_align_v1_7to1
variants.description: Tighter TP variant. 7:1 R:R (10.5x ATR TP). The KAMA Stoch family achieved PPR 4.268 at 8:1 — testing 7:1 for higher win rate at the cost of smaller winners. If KAMA's filtering produces cleaner entries with less overshoot, tighter TP captures more reliable exits.

components.notes: Tighter 7:1 R:R for higher win rate.

advisory_context.novel_element: ALIGNMENT (not divergence) between KAMA and Vortex — opposite of failed kama_vortex_divergence (0/9 WR). Fuses #2 promoted family (KAMA, PPR 4.27) with PF champion (Vortex, PF 2.034). KAMA's adaptive speed acts as natural regime filter.

indicator_roles.baseline: KAMA_10_2_30 — Kaufman Adaptive MA, speed-adapts to noise, natural choppy-market filter
indicator_roles.confirmation: VTXP_14/VTXM_14 — Vortex crossover for directional momentum transition detection (PF=2.034 champion)
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
